pattern_summary: AI pressure-tests a CANDIDATE trade BEFORE entry: user describes the setup (or selects a saved playbook + fills in current values), AI returns a go/no-go + risk-flags + historical-comparison ('your last 10 of this setup: 4W/6L, all losses came when X was true; X is true now'). Reverses the entire industry's post-mortem stance. Only TradeJournal.AI ships this as a named button in 2026 ('Ask AI on this setup'). Green-field opportunity for any journal with a structured playbook substrate — pre-trade validation is just the playbook's entry-conditions run as a checklist, plus a historical lookup, plus a one-paragraph LLM synthesis.

ai_features.feature_name: Closest live examples: TradeJournal.AI 'Ask AI / Pressure-test'. SMB-style mental-pre-trade-checklist (manual). Bellafiore PlayBook-checkup adapted for pre-trade. No widely-marketed pattern-name yet — opportunity to coin one ('PlayCheck' / 'Pre-Trade Coach' / 'GoCheck').
ai_features.ai_scope: Per-candidate-trade BEFORE entry — analysis unit is a not-yet-executed setup.
ai_features.ai_proactive_vs_reactive: REACTIVE (user clicks 'pressure-test this' from entry-modal). Could become PROACTIVE on hosted+live exchanges with browser-extension watching DOM, but that crosses into trading-signal territory — explicitly out-of-scope for a journal.
ai_features.ai_minimum_trade_threshold: Needs >=10 historical trades on the SAME playbook to deliver meaningful 'your past stats say X'. Below that, falls back to rule-checklist-only mode ('we don't have history yet, but your defined entry-conditions are: [list]. {N}/{total} are met right now.').
ai_features.ai_underlying_tech: LLM (Claude/GPT-4) + structured lookup over the user's playbook + trade-history table. Optional: live market-data feed (price / funding / OI for crypto-perps; sector / VIX for stocks) but data-source cost grows fast.
ai_features.output_format: Structured card with three sections: (1) PLAYBOOK CHECKLIST (visual checks: green/amber/red per condition), (2) HISTORICAL COMPARISON (this looks most like {N} prior trades, their outcome was {result}), (3) LLM SYNTHESIS (1-paragraph go/no-go with the single most important risk to monitor).
ai_features.notable_prompts: (1) 'Given setup {playbook_name} with current conditions {filled_values}, lookup user's history. Return {n_match, hit_rate_match, common_failure_mode}.' (2) 'Pressure-test this trade: argue the BEAR case in 3 bullets. What's the single reason this trade fails?' (3) 'Compare current conditions to user's BEST 5 and WORST 5 instances of this playbook. Which set does it resemble more?' (4) Bellafiore-style: 'Pre-trade variable check: for each of the 5 SMB variables, current-state vs play-required-state. <4/5 met = size down or skip.'

playbook_depth.playbook_concept_maturity: Pre-trade validation requires structured-rules level minimum — without explicit entry-conditions there's nothing to check. AI-coached level adds: 'your stated rules are these, but your WINNING trades reveal an unstated rule [X] that's actually doing the work — make it explicit'.
playbook_depth.setup_level_ai: Tightly coupled — the same playbook entity used for setup-level coaching is the substrate for pre-trade validation. Build one, get both.
playbook_depth.pre_trade_validation: This IS the pattern. Implementation: each playbook's entry-conditions become a runnable checklist on the entry-modal; user fills/auto-fills current values; AI checks; user gates or proceeds.

coaching_depth.coaching_depth_level: deep_pattern_analysis when history exists, structured_checklist when cold. Most powerful because it intervenes BEFORE the mistake — Steenbarger argues this is 100x more behavior-changing than post-mortem analysis.

concrete_implementations: TradeJournal.AI 'Ask AI' button: reads past trades on same setup, gives pre-entry synthesis. Sole named implementation as of mid-2026.
Bellafiore PlayBook 5-variable check (manual, paper-and-pen at SMB desk): the ANALOG version that informs the pattern.
RizeTrade entry-checklist (no AI, structured-rules-only): the foundation other journals could AI-ify.
Process Trader 'hard rules + pre-market routines': adjacent pattern, focuses on discipline-rules not setup-specific validation.
Plancana 'AI-generated rules': inverts the flow (AI proposes rules), close cousin but not pre-trade-per-candidate.
critique: Three risks: (1) BECOMES A TRADING-SIGNAL — if AI says 'don't take this', that's advice, regulatory grey area in some jurisdictions; mitigate with 'this is reflection-help, not financial advice' framing and never give buy/sell/size recs, only checklist-status. (2) USER-FRICTION — adds 30 seconds to every entry; fast scalpers will bypass it. Mitigate with one-click 'auto-fill from current market' + skip-toggle. (3) DATA-FRESHNESS — if checklist requires live funding-rate / OI you need real-time data; without it the AI is reasoning over stale data and gives wrong reads. Mitigate by being explicit about which fields are auto-filled vs user-filled.
opportunity_gap_crypto_perp: Perfect-fit niche: most pre-trade checks for stocks are SUBJECTIVE (is the macro right? is sector strong?) — hard to auto-fill. For crypto-perps the SAME checks are NUMERICAL and live (funding-rate is a number, OI is a number, liquidation-clusters are a number, BTC.D is a number). A pre-trade check that auto-fills 'BTC.D rising = NO, your playbook requires falling', 'funding>0.02% = your playbook's 7-trade fail-rate was 71%', 'liquidation-cluster within 1% above entry = TP3 likely capped' is genuinely UNIQUE. No major competitor offers this in 2026.
uncertain: Exact UX of TradeJournal.AI's 'Ask AI' button (whether it's pre-trade or only post-trade) — needs product walkthrough to verify
Whether any prop-firm (FTMO, Funding Pips) ships a similar pre-trade AI internally to enforce risk rules
